test.command: PYTEST_ADDOPTS='-p no:pytest_ethereum' BROWNIE_NETWORK=mainnet .venv/bin/pytest tests/test_constants.py tests/test_time.py tests/test_dank_import_guard.py tests/test_price_result.py tests/test_trade_path_sources.py tests/test_cachebox.py -W ignore -s --tb=short > /tmp/test_full_baseline_erc4626.log 2>&1
test.notes: 97 tests passed in 1.05s. NOTE: The initial run found 1 failure (test_no_unbounded_memory_caches) due to cache_type='memory' being on a separate line from ram_cache_maxsize in y/prices/erc4626.py. Fixed in commit f246ab0d by consolidating to a single line. All 97 tests pass after fix. ERC4626 mainnet tests (tests/test_erc4626.py) require ETHERSCAN_TOKEN env var and are run separately.

typecheck.notes: mypy output shows pre-existing errors in other modules (y/_db/log.py, y/datatypes.py, y/utils/logging.py) but no new errors introduced by erc4626.py. All errors are pre-existing codebase patterns consistent with other milestone scrutiny validators.

lint.command: N/A - ruff and flake8 not installed in project venv
lint.notes: No linter installed. Code follows existing project conventions.

appliedUpdates.description: Added note to architecture.md documenting has_methods() limitation: cannot check methods requiring input parameters. Only no-input view methods should be used for detection.

Fixed test failure: consolidated @a_sync.a_sync(cache_type='memory', ...) decorator to a single line in y/prices/erc4626.py (commit f246ab0d). test_no_unbounded_memory_caches was failing because it checks per-line.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: cache_type='memory' and ram_cache_maxsize must appear on the SAME LINE in @a_sync.a_sync() decorators. The test_no_unbounded_memory_caches test checks per-line, not per-decorator. Always write: @a_sync.a_sync(default='sync', cache_type='memory', ram_cache_ttl=..., ram_cache_maxsize=ENVS.DEFAULT_CACHE_MAXSIZE) on a single line.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: erc4626-vaults worker split the decorator across multiple lines, causing test_no_unbounded_memory_caches to fail during scrutiny validation. All other files in the codebase (exotic_tokens.py, yearn.py, magic.py, etc.) use single-line format.

rejectedObservations.observation: SQLite DB cache may have stale entries for tokens that were classified before erc4626 bucket was added
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous - this is a runtime/operational concern, not actionable by workers; no clear resolution without production DB access

featureReviews.summary: ERC4626 vault detection and pricing correctly implemented. is_erc4626_vault() uses has_methods(asset(), totalAssets()) with @optional_async_diskcache and single-line cache decorator (after fix). get_price() uses previewRedeem with convertToAssets fallback and returns None if both revert. @stuck_coro_debugger on get_price(). Bucket registered in buckets.py calls_only dict. Dispatch in magic.py with descriptive source string 'ERC4626 vault {sym} underlying {asset_sym} via previewRedeem'. 9 tests cover all major scenarios. One bug fixed by scrutiny validator: multi-line cache decorator consolidated to single line.
nonBlockingIssues: Detection uses asset() + totalAssets() instead of asset() + previewRedeem(uint256) as originally specified (VAL-4626-001). This is due to has_methods() limitation - it cannot check input-parameter methods. The detection still correctly identifies ERC4626 vaults per the test results.
Cache decorator was initially split across multiple lines, failing test_no_unbounded_memory_caches. Fixed by scrutiny validator in commit f246ab0d.
